platform.name: PulseOps V2
platform.welcomeSubtitle: Modular operations platform with hot-drop micro-frontends
platform.noModuleSelected: Select a module from the navigation to get started.

auth.loginSubtitle: Enter your credentials to access the platform
auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.

login.failed: Login failed. Please check your credentials.
login.networkError: Unable to connect to the server. Please check if the API is running.

login.title: Welcome Back
login.subtitle: Sign in to your account
login.superAdminHint: To access SuperAdmin, enter "SuperAdmin" as the username.
login.validationError: Both email/username and password are required

superAdminLogin.title: Super Administrator
superAdminLogin.subtitle: Platform SuperAdmin — Restricted Access
superAdminLogin.notice: This login is for the designated SuperAdmin only. All other users should use the standard login.

dashboard.title: Platform Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: System health, module status, and quick actions

moduleManager.title: Module Manager
moduleManager.subtitle: Install, enable, disable, and manage platform modules

available.title: Available Modules
available.subtitle: Modules discovered in the hot-drop folder. Install to register.
available.empty: No modules found in the hot-drop folder. Build a module first.

installed.title: Installed Modules
installed.subtitle: Modules registered in the platform. Enable to activate.
installed.empty: No modules installed yet. Scan for available modules first.

confirm.enableMessage: This will load the module's API routes and make it available in the platform.
confirm.disableMessage: This will unload the module's API routes. The module will no longer be accessible.
confirm.removeMessage: This will remove the module from the platform registry. The bundle files will remain in dist-modules/.
confirm.installMessage: This will register the module in the platform. You can enable it afterwards.

toast.enableSuccess: Module enabled successfully. API routes loaded.
toast.actionFailed: Module action failed. Check API logs.

logs.title: Platform Admin — Logs
logs.subtitle: View, search, and manage platform UI and API logs

stats.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all logs from the configured source? This action cannot be undone.
stats.noLogsHint: Logs will appear here as the platform generates activity.

detail.title: Log Details
detail.noSelection: Select a log entry to view details

dbConnection.title: Database Connection
dbConnection.subtitle: Real-time connection status to the configured PostgreSQL database

superAdminAuth.title: SuperAdmin Credentials
superAdminAuth.subtitle: Update the SuperAdmin account password. Changes take effect on next login.
superAdminAuth.newPasswordPlaceholder: Minimum 12 characters, mixed case, digit and special char
superAdminAuth.passwordMismatch: New password and confirmation do not match.
superAdminAuth.passwordWeak: Password must be at least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
superAdminAuth.requirePasswordChangeNotice: A password change is required. Please update your SuperAdmin password.

databaseSetup.title: Database Setup
databaseSetup.subtitle: Create database, initialize schema, manage default data and RBAC tables

logConfig.title: Log Configuration
logConfig.subtitle: Configure database logging settings, log level, capture options, and management
logConfig.enabledDesc: When disabled, no logs are captured or persisted to the database.
logConfig.logLevelDesc: Only Logs at or above this level will be captured.
logConfig.dbStatusDesc: Verify that the database is ready to receive log entries.
logConfig.captureApiLogsDesc: Capture API request and response logs
logConfig.dbNotReady: Database logging cannot be enabled until database is reachable and database tables for logging are setup.
logConfig.dbNotReadyDesc: Database must be configured and initialized before enabling logging.

logSettings.title: Log Storage Settings
logSettings.subtitle: Configure where platform logs are stored
logSettings.fileModeDesc: Logs are written to JSON files in the API project's Logs folder. Default mode.
logSettings.dbModeDesc: Logs are persisted to the PostgreSQL database for centralized querying.
logSettings.dbNotReady: Database logging cannot be enabled until database is reachable and database tables for logging are setup.
logSettings.dbNotReadyDesc: Database must be configured and initialized before enabling database logging.

authSettings.title: Authentication Settings
authSettings.subtitle: Configure the authentication provider for regular platform users. SuperAdmin always authenticates via JSON file.

database.label: Database
database.description: Authenticate against the PostgreSQL system_users table with bcrypt hashing.
database.detail: Requires the database to be initialized with default data. Switch here after setup.

social.label: Social / OAuth2 / SAML
social.description: Authenticate via Google, Microsoft, or other OAuth2/SAML providers.
social.detail: Requires OAuth2/SAML provider configuration. Available in a future release.

authSettings.dbNotReadyWarning: Database must be initialized before switching to Database authentication.
authSettings.dbRequiredNote: Set up the database in Settings → Database Objects first.

switchProvider.confirmMessage: This will change the authentication provider for the entire platform. All users will authenticate using the new provider.

logs.emptyHint: Logs will appear here as you interact with the platform.

apiCalls.emptyHint: API requests and responses will appear here.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete All Logs
deleteConfirm.actionDescription: permanently delete all captured logs and API call records

errors.networkError: Unable to connect to the server. Please check your connection.
errors.unauthorized: You are not authorized to perform this action.
errors.notFound: The requested resource was not found.
errors.serverError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errors.moduleLoadFailed: Failed to load module. Please try again or contact support.

databaseObjects.title: Database Objects
databaseObjects.subtitle: Manage database, schema, and default data

database.title: Database
database.createDesc: Create the PostgreSQL database if it does not exist.
database.deleteDesc: Drop the entire database. This is destructive and cannot be undone.

schema.title: Schema & Tables
schema.createDesc: Create the core schema and tables (system_users, system_config, system_modules, system_logs).
schema.wipeDesc: Drop all tables in the schema. Data will be permanently lost.
